name: Primary Genre
description: The dominant gameplay genre that best characterizes the game's core mechanics.

values.name: Shooter
values.description: FPS, TPS, battle royale, or tactical military combat games where shooting is the core action.

values.name: RPG
values.description: Role-playing games including JRPGs, CRPGs, action-RPGs, and roguelite RPGs with character progression and stats.

values.name: Strategy & Management
values.description: 4X, grand strategy, RTS, tycoon, city-builders, and management/business sims.

values.name: Simulation
values.description: Vehicle, life, farming, job, or sandbox simulators focused on emulating an activity or system.

values.name: Action & Platformer
values.description: Action-adventure, fighting games, metroidvanias, platformers, and hack-and-slash titles.

values.name: Puzzle & Party
values.description: Puzzle games, party brawlers, physics chaos games, and minigame collections.

values.name: Narrative & Adventure
values.description: Visual novels, point-and-click adventures, walking sims, and story-driven exploration games.

values.name: Survival & Sandbox
values.description: Survival crafting, sandbox builders, and open-ended exploration/extraction games.

values.name: Other
values.description: None of the above values clearly fit. When picking Other, also provide a 2-5 word phrase in the corresponding _other field describing what would actually fit (e.g., 'rhythm game', 'visual novel-music hybrid').

name: Setting & Aesthetic
description: The fictional world, era, or cultural style the game evokes.

values.name: Contemporary Real-World
values.description: Modern-day Earth settings: cities, jobs, sports, cars, and present-day military scenarios.

values.name: Historical
values.description: Set in a specific real historical period such as WWII, ancient Rome, medieval Europe, or the Cold War.

values.name: High Fantasy
values.description: Western fantasy realms with magic, dragons, dungeons, and medieval-style worlds.

values.name: Sci-Fi & Space
values.description: Futuristic, cyberpunk, space exploration, or alien-world settings.

values.name: Anime & East Asian
values.description: Japanese, Chinese, or Korean-inspired worlds: wuxia, samurai, anime aesthetics, and JRPG fantasy.

values.name: Horror & Dark Atmospheric
values.description: Gothic, post-apocalyptic, dystopian, haunted, or psychological horror settings.

values.name: Cozy & Whimsical
values.description: Cute, pastoral, cartoony, or lighthearted worlds like farms, slime ranches, and party-game arenas.

values.name: Abstract & Stylized
values.description: Non-representational, geometric, retro-pixel, or tool/utility apps without a concrete setting.

values.name: Other
values.description: None of the above values clearly fit. When picking Other, also provide a 2-5 word phrase in the corresponding _other field describing what would actually fit (e.g., 'mythology-Pacific Islander', 'cosmic horror underwater').

name: Player Mode
description: Whether the game is built primarily for solo play, multiplayer, or both.

values.name: Single-Player Focused
values.description: Designed primarily as a solo experience with little or no multiplayer component.

values.name: Co-op Friendly
values.description: Single-player or story-driven games that prominently support cooperative play with friends.

values.name: Competitive Multiplayer
values.description: PvP-centric games like shooters, fighting games, MOBAs, and competitive sports titles.

values.name: Massively Multiplayer
values.description: MMOs, large-scale battle royales, and persistent-world games supporting many simultaneous players.

values.name: Local & Party
values.description: Designed around couch co-op, party play, or chaotic same-screen multiplayer.

values.name: Other
values.description: None of the above values clearly fit. When picking Other, also provide a 2-5 word phrase in the corresponding _other field describing what would actually fit (e.g., 'asynchronous PvP', 'spectator only', 'not a game').

name: Pacing & Intensity
description: The typical moment-to-moment tempo and stress level of play.

values.name: Twitchy & Action-Packed
values.description: Fast reflexes, real-time combat, bullet hell, or high-APM gameplay.

values.name: Tense & Suspenseful
values.description: Horror, hardcore survival, or stealth games where dread and high stakes dominate.

values.name: Tactical & Deliberate
values.description: Turn-based, strategic, or methodical games rewarding careful planning over reflexes.

values.name: Steady & Progression-Driven
values.description: RPGs, sims, and builders with continuous moderate-pace activity and long-term goals.

values.name: Relaxed & Cozy
values.description: Low-pressure games meant to soothe: farming, ranching, walking sims, casual puzzles.

values.name: Chaotic & Silly
values.description: Slapstick physics, party games, and absurdist sandboxes built around hilarity over challenge.

values.name: Other
values.description: None of the above values clearly fit. When picking Other, also provide a 2-5 word phrase in the corresponding _other field describing what would actually fit (e.g., 'meditative ambient', 'rhythm-locked tempo').

name: Session Structure
description: How a typical play session is shaped and what players take away from it.

values.name: Linear Campaign
values.description: Story-driven games with a defined beginning, middle, and end played through once or twice.

values.name: Open-World Exploration
values.description: Large persistent worlds where players freely roam, quest, and discover at their own pace.

values.name: Run-Based Roguelike
values.description: Short repeatable runs with permadeath, procedural generation, and meta-progression.

values.name: Match-Based Sessions
values.description: Discrete rounds or matches like shooters, sports, fighting, and party games.

values.name: Sandbox & Creative
values.description: Open-ended building, crafting, or simulation with no fixed endpoint or win condition.

values.name: Other
values.description: None of the above values clearly fit. When picking Other, also provide a 2-5 word phrase in the corresponding _other field describing what would actually fit (e.g., 'episodic releases', 'live-service seasons').
